post: A huge congratulations to Master Printing + Mailing for partnering with the Bill of Rights Institute to support the Power of the Printed Word initiative  donating the printing and distribution of 250,000 pocket Constitutions to 2,500 classrooms across the country. Let that sink in. In a time when civics education has never been more important, this effort helps place our founding documents directly into the hands of the next generation. That is impact. That is leadership. And that is the power of print. This initiative is a reminder that our industry does more than produce materials  we help preserve history, strengthen communities, and support education at the highest level. We also applaud the incredible Graphic Media Alliance partners who stepped up to support this effort: Konica Minolta Business Solutions U. S. A., Inc., Heidelberg North America, Case Paper, History By Mail, TECNAU, and Canon USA. When suppliers and printers align around purpose, powerful things happen. To Master Printing + Mailing  thank you for leading by example. Your generosity and commitment to meaningful work represent the very best of our industry. If you want to see print change the world, this is it.  #PowerOfThePrintedWord  #CivicsEducation  #PrintMatters  #GraphicCommunications  #IndustryLeadership

We are proud to support the Bill of Rights Institute's Power of the Printed Word Initiative by donating the printing and distribution of "250 for 250." That's 250,000 pocket constitutions supporting 2,500 government, history and civics classrooms across the country! The Constitution and Bill of Rights remain the framework and firm foundation of the American experiment. If you are interested in learning more, please visit the Bill of Rights Institute at www.mybri.org  #constitution  #billofrights  #civics  #history  #government  #poweroftheprintedword  #printing  #marketing  #digitalmarketing  #printwithmaster

The fight for women's rights in America didn't begin in 1848. It began in 1776. Under the coverture system, American women existed largely through the legal identities of their husbands and fathers. Yet the Declaration of Independence, the same document that launched a revolution, became the tool women wielded in their centuries-long struggle for liberty and equality. 250 years later, that story is still being told. And it deserves to be told well. This March, the Bill of Rights Institute is partnering with political scientist and educator Savannah Eccles Johnston, PhD (@savypolitics) for a Women's History Month series: The Women's Declaration, tracing the arc of American women's fight from founding mothers like Abigail Adams and Mercy Otis Warren, to suffragists like Elizabeth Cady Stanton and Susan B. Anthony, to revolutionaries like Alice Paul and Ida B. Wells, to the legal minds who argued equality before the highest courts in the land. This is the story of American women. It's also the story of America itself, and the struggle to fulfill its most fundamental promise. Episode 1 is live now 👇 🎬  https://bit.ly/4u56DDp  #WomensHistoryMonth  #America250  #CivicsEducation  #AmericanHistory  #TeacherResources

The student running a small business at your school? The class that organized a community food drive? The club advocating for safer roads? They're already doing it. The MyImpact Challenge is a national civics contest, but it's probably not what you're picturing. It isn't a protest sign or a voter registration table. It's students taking meaningful action in their communities: through entrepreneurship, philanthropy, service, and problem-solving. That means the business club renting out equipment at prom. The Key Club running a book drive. The FFA chapter cleaning up a park. The AP Gov class that spent a semester pushing for safer teen driving laws in their town. All of it qualifies. Students in all 50 states can enter. Every student who submits a valid project receives recognition, not just finalists. And the national winner earns a scholarship and a platform to share their story. If you work with students who are making a difference, or who could, the next step is straightforward. 👉  https://bit.ly/3fyGGr3  #MyImpactChallenge  #CivicsEducation  #ServiceLearning  #BillOfRightsInstitute  #TeacherPD

Teachers: give us your best 50 words on a memorable America at 250 classroom moment. Youll be entered in our weekly Civic Star Challenge prize drawing for up to $10,000). Tip screens are everywhere, 18%, 20%, 25%. Should the U. S. abolish tipping and shift pay into transparent prices and wages? Students can submit a Yes/No/In-between response by March 12. Whats your take?  https://bit.ly/3MPDtr1  #ThinkTheVote  #CivicEducation  #CivilDiscourse  #Economics

Quick class hook: Lincolns Gettysburg Address (1863) reframed the Civil War around equality + self-government. After the video, ask: What is the ‘unfinished work, and whos responsible for it?   https://bit.ly/4awbUMF  #GettysburgAddress  #CivicEducation
